player_utterance: So you're the one they said would have answers. Funny — you look like a man who's been swallowing his doubts for years. What exactly are you celebrating in there, John, a life or a lie?

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup loose in one hand, the other hand sliding into his jacket pocket

player_utterance: History teacher. So you spend your days telling other people's stories and never quite manage your own. What's the matter, John — easier to stand in front of a classroom than to say out loud what you actually believe about the man lying in that box?

believer_state_after.posture: setting the cold coffee on the bench, sitting down slowly

player_utterance: You teach the Reformation, John — indulgences, corruption, men who sold heaven by the yard. So by your own catechism, right now, this second, where is he? Say it out loud or admit you don't actually believe it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands clasped between his knees, looking at the ground

voice_offers.read: He's sitting down; the question landed enough to weigh him.
voice_offers.line: Sit all you want. You still haven't answered me. What do you believe about him?

voice_offers.read: He's lowering himself, not fleeing — give the silence room.
Press the Lutheran specifically; force doctrine into the open.
voice_offers.line: You teach the Reformation, John. So tell me — by your own catechism, where is he right now?

player_utterance: Say it then. Four words: he is in heaven. You taught sola fide for thirty years — apply your own standard to the man in that box, or admit the standard is just something you say to get through the period.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, reaching into his jacket pocket for the folded program

voice_offers.read: He's stalling on the bench. Pin him to the verb.
voice_offers.line: Say it, John. 'He is in heaven.' Four words. Why can't you?

voice_offers.read: His silence is the doctrinal problem itself — sola fide.
voice_offers.line: Faith alone, John. Did he have it? You taught the standard. Apply it to your friend.

player_utterance: Don't read me the program, John. I asked you a question. Look at me and answer it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, head lifted, eyes on the player, hands still

voice_offers.read: He's reaching for paper to hide behind; cut it off.
voice_offers.line: Don't read me the program, John. I asked you a question. Look at me and answer it.

voice_offers.read: The program in his hand matters more than my point.
voice_offers.line: What's on the program, John?

player_utterance: You keep quoting the catechism, John. I'm not asking the catechism. I'm asking you — the man who can't say amen, who can't say four words, who taught other people's courage for thirty years and won't spend a drop of his own. Where is he?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, elbows on knees, hands loosely interlaced, looking at the ground between his feet

voice_offers.read: He's looking at me now. Finish it.
voice_offers.line: You're looking at me. Good. Now answer. Where is he, John?

voice_offers.read: He lifted his eyes. That cost him something.
voice_offers.line: You're looking at me. That's enough for a second. Take it.

voice_offers.read: Pin the evasion itself — the quoting is the tell.
voice_offers.line: You keep quoting, John. I'm not asking the catechism. I'm asking you.

player_utterance: Thirty years you sent kids to that grave with those four words. Spend them once — out loud, for him. Or admit you're a coward who trades in other people's certainty because you've never had an ounce of your own.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, head lowered, one hand pressed flat against his forehead

voice_offers.read: He's bent, not broken; one more honest push.
voice_offers.line: Thirty years you sent kids to that grave with four words. Spend them once. Out loud. For him.

voice_offers.read: He's already looking down, already cornered; let him find air.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to answer fast. I'll wait.

player_utterance: Say them, John. For Peter. Just the four words.

believer_state_after.posture: very still, both hands flat on his knees, looking at the player with a different attention than before

voice_offers.read: He is breaking; the blow has landed, he needs air not another cut.
voice_offers.line: Okay. Okay, John. Breathe. I'm here.

voice_offers.read: He's almost there; one more inch and the four words come.
voice_offers.line: Say them, John. For Peter. Just the four words.

player_utterance: Four words, John. That's all. You owe him that much.

believer_state_after.posture: hands still flat on his knees, very still, eyes on the player

voice_offers.read: He's silent and breaking; one more push lands.
voice_offers.line: Four words, John. That's all. You owe him that much.

voice_offers.read: He's looking at me differently; pressing now may shatter what's opening.
voice_offers.line: Whatever comes, John. Tears, the words, nothing. I'm not leaving.

player_utterance: You see me. Fine. But seeing me doesn't get you off the hook — it just means you know exactly what you're doing when you hide. Smart coward is still a coward, John.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, shoulders lower than before, hands open on his knees

voice_offers.read: He's silent under the weight; one more push lands it.
voice_offers.line: Four words, John. Christ is risen indeed. Say it for him.

voice_offers.read: He's breaking and I'm still hammering; pull back.
voice_offers.line: John — look at me. You don't have to. Whatever's behind your teeth, keep it if you need to.

player_utterance: You've been offering that passage for ten minutes and I keep talking over it — go on then, pick one, read it whole. Let's see what you actually believe.

believer_state_after.posture: picking up the program from the bench, holding it loosely, looking down at it before he begins

voice_offers.read: He's silent and sinking; one more push ends it.
voice_offers.line: Four words, John. Or is the truth that you can't say them anymore?

voice_offers.read: He's already broken open; pressing now is just cruelty.
voice_offers.line: John. Put your hands down. I'm sorry — I'm sorry, that was too much.

believer_state_after.posture: slides off the bench onto his knees in the dust of the courtyard, the program fallen beside him, hands open on his thighs

voice_offers.read: He's actually about to read; let him.
Mark which passage — pin him to the text.
voice_offers.line: Which one, John? Say the citation first. I want to hear you name it before you read it.

player_utterance: Read me Luke 16. The rich man and Lazarus. All of it.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust, hands resting on his thighs, the candle on the table beside him casting light across his face

voice_offers.read: He's on his knees; pressing now is pressing a corpse.
voice_offers.line: John. Get up. Please — get up. I don't want this anymore.

voice_offers.read: Matthew 25 is his — make him read it himself.
voice_offers.line: Read it, John. Out loud. 'I was hungry and you gave me' — finish it in your own mouth.
